title: Response #2 to Hardcore Muslim about God, Muslim Immigration & Overpopulation

transcript.text: Okay, so this is part of religious discussion with the hardcore Muslim.
I got a response email from the person.
So, I'll send a response video again.
And we can go back and forth and talk about all the different things that we,
All the different points that we had to make.
So, the person wrote me back and said that he didn't agree.
He said that we do treat our celebrities like gods,
And that's not good.
And I said, I didn't mention that everybody was a part of God, everybody's God.
And he said, well, you know, Hitler wasn't God.
Well, I think Hitler, Hitler was a person too, and he was just a victim of circumstance.
There are a lot of people who believe like him and, you know, reincarnate and reincarnate
And eventually get to go to heaven just like the rest of us.
And it's not to, it's not to, like, put him in a place of God.
Like, there's God, which is the grand, like,
You know, that force that we're all a part of.
And as you go higher and higher up, we kind of become views into this one soul,
And we're all little bits of it.
And, okay, so the person took issue.
I asked them what country they're from, and they didn't tell me, but I think they're from Saudi Arabia
Because I get a lot of views from Saudi Arabia.
And they're obviously not a native English speaker, so, yeah.
Now, he didn't like the fat part about the video where I said that Muslims are going to,
Or moving to, like, Scandinavia and then multiplying like rabbits and, you know, bringing their religion there.
And he said, he said that it's like that, well, he said that they were all, I'll include his,
What he wrote on the comment section of the video because it's not too big, I don't think.
At least I'll put most of it on there.
But he said that they come from there.
And, well, no, those Muslims don't come from there.
That's what I was saying.
And when you want to, Islam is good, but not when you want to push it on other people.
Those Muslims went there.
The people who originally from Northern Europe are pagans and Christians,
The Muslims went there as refugees.
And, but he was defending that they were breeding like rabbits.
And that because he's saying that a lot of European towns are abandoned
Because there's not enough youth and that he even said that they don't want to raise,
White people don't want to raise their youth because they're all brats and they don't like them.
And so I guess it's up for them, up to the Muslims to move into Europe and procreate
Because they're the only ones who want to.
And that Japan has to have robots take care of their, their old people
Because there's not enough people there.
And this actually brings up a really interesting point
Because a lot of people, in fact, somebody did a video about, like, my philosophy that I wrote on YouTube.
The other day, I YouTubed myself and a couple of people did some videos about me.
I'll include the links on this video.
But, and, but I said, you know, everybody should express themselves and that'll fix the world.
And he said, no, that's not the problem.
The world is overpopulation.
And that is one of the problems with the world, overpopulation.
We're just using up the resources and hurting the environment,
Which will then cause us to have an economic collapse.
It's all caused by overpopulation.
So we don't need more people.
But what the person brought up is really interesting is that when a country gets to a certain level of development,
We don't have overpopulation problems because we don't breed as many children
Because people kind of have the leisure activity to, you know, have fun and go on vacation
And, you know, have leisure time and go, you know, running and stuff.
And we don't know where to populate.
And so when the whole world gets to be, you know, a certain level of economic stability,
We can control the population.
And so we don't need Muslims to multiply like rabbits to save us
Because we're not, because all of our children are brats.
And we don't want to raise our kids.
The only people who love their kids enough and want to raise children are Muslims.
That's not accurate.
Now, he also said that he didn't, he still says that science is bad, that science is anti-God.
Well, let me explain this again, okay?
Maybe I didn't get it right the first time.
Science is good.
It's not anti-God.
But science is what gave us the technology which made our life simpler or easier
And allowed us to control our population and control the world and make the world a better place.
The world is a better place than it was before.
It's not getting worse.
We're a lot more humane.
We don't have slavery anymore.
We don't have wars all the time.
We're not as brutal to each other.
There's not as much class difference.
We don't have, you know, as much violence.
We're more educated.
We know more.
We get to travel around more.
The world's a better place.
But that's because of science.
Science is what got us to the moon.
You know, we have to follow the laws of science.
We can't just do whatever we want, you know?
You can't, you have to follow, you know, like if you drive too fast, you're going to crash.
That's why we have speed limits.
That's what science is.
It's following laws and just, you know, doing math to figure out what you need to, you know, to do to get the result.
And that's what science is.
It doesn't have to be anti-God.
It's just, it's pro-God.
It's just knowing the laws of God and following Him.
And that's not anti-Islam anyways.
Islam back in, you know, the 10 hundreds was the most scientific religion in the world.
They were the ones who were, who devised the size of the earth based on scientific books that they had translated from ancient Greek.
You know, in ancient Greek, I think it was Aristophanes devised the size of the earth to within 200 miles back in 400s BC.
And then the Muslims translated that book and did the scientific experiment and came up with the same, the same conclusion.
They knew the world was round before the Christians did.
They were doing eye surgery and studying anatomy and all this stuff, all this scientific stuff.
It was Muslim.
Muslims were doing this.
That's why, you know, we got, you know, the Arabic numerals.
That's why we call them Arabic numerals.
Before that in Europe, we were using Roman numerals.
We didn't have the zero.
We couldn't do calculations.
And we learned that from, from the Arabs, from the Muslims who learned it from the Indians.
But, so it's not, science is not anti-Muslim.
You just, you should study history.
And you'll find that out.
That's why, you know, algebra, that's math.
What we use for science?
Al-gebra.
I think you even wrote that in your first email to me.
From Al-Hadin or whatever.
There are lots of those.
Um, alchemy, you know.
The ancestor of chemistry came from them.
And, uh, so it's the sharing of knowledge.
They, they learned it from the Greeks and the Indians and, and, uh, you know, so learning and communicating in science, that's a good thing.
And it's not anti-Muslim.
Um, it's, it's anti, um, right-wing fundamentalist Muslim.
Now, one thing he said was kind of weird.
He said, if I want to call someone to Islam, I will call someone who will do what I teach him.
If I teach him how to fly a plane into a building, then that's what he will do.
If I teach him how to pray, then that's what he will do.
So, so your idea of Islam is basically one person telling another person what to believe and what to do, and then they do it.
And so it's some kind of, like, uh, autocratic system, kind of, where the king tells everybody else what to do.
Well, that is, like, what you're saying God worship for an American's is, where we worship our Vinny, you know, from, from the, uh, from the entourage.
And we worship our, um, uh, celebrities and actors and stuff like they're God.
Well, that's what you guys do if you say that.
If I tell you, you know, you're only Islam if you have to fly into a plane because I tell you to do.
And you have to pray.
That's, that is looking at somebody like they're God.
They're your God, and they do what you want.
Um, and that's not the God that I'm saying, and that's not the God of the, of the, uh, of, uh, you know, star worship or whatever, adulation, and stars making lots of money.
That's just our economic system, and that's how it works.
People gain money that they're, that they're worth.
You know, like, people choose to pay $300 a ticket to watch baseball games, and the stadiums can seat 50,000 people,
And the teams that win are the ones who get the, the people watching the games on TV and in the stadiums.
So then they need to have the best players, and the way that they're going to get the best players is by paying the most money.
That's just how it works.
Um, now I think it's ridiculous, but then again, I'm not one of these people spending $300 a ticket.
It's, the, the cause of all the ridiculousness is the people who are paying the ticket, but that's giving responsibility to individuals.
That's not having one guy tell another guy what he's going to do.
And so, you know, so let's see, did you make any other points?
Yes, you said astrology.
Well, astrology doesn't deserve the slightest attention than other beings, some silly thing to laugh at.
Um, yeah, Christians, you know, uh, funnily enough Christians would say the same thing about astrology.

But, astrology is just an ancient knowledge based on science from back a long time ago from the ancient Indians, you know, the Vedas, um, who learned that from the aliens who were the ultimate scientists.
And let me explain the science of astrology really quickly.

The, the, the alignment of the planets does affect, uh, the physicality here on the earth in the same way that when you're in a, um, if you want to have a good cell signal, you don't want to be on the other side of the mountain.
You want to be on top of the mountain where the cell signal can reach you.
And when you line up all the different towers, they would have them all lined up so the energy can pass through.
And all of these planets and stars are like energy beacons that pick up energy and transmit and pass it.
Because there are these big objects, you know, made out of certain material out in the middle of space that communicate with each other with energy we can't see.
That's how cell phones work.
They're communicating with radio waves that we can't see.
And we, and we, you know, we developed all that kind of stuff with science, you know.
I don't know what you think, you know, the computer you're using, like, was created, but it was created from all these different laws of science.
And all of these rules of math.
And you wouldn't be able to do it if it wasn't for science, you know.
I mean, if the Muslims were to kill all of the Naz Muslims in the world, the hardcore right-wing Muslims,
They wouldn't be able to continue living with all the technology they have.
Without using the same scientific principles, they'd have to have scientists.
And if they don't, they're just going to bring themselves back to the Dark Age.
And, you know, in the end, science is power, you know.
The Israelites can, if they wanted to, they could kill every last Muslim in the Middle East in a second if they wanted to.
They've got the nuclear bomb, neutron bomb, scalar bomb.
They've got all of them.
And they made that stuff with science, you know.
They do the experiments out in the desert.
They figure out how it goes.
And they invent things.
And so, you know, it's not about, it's not about, it doesn't have to be against God.
In fact, those people believe in God.
Einstein believed in God.
He had a quote.
Science without God is lame and God without science.
No, no, science without God is, like, crippled and science without, God without science is lame or something like that.
So, anyways, yeah.
Those are the points that we can talk about if you want to write me.
I won't.
You didn't comment on my last video, so I won't mention your YouTube name.
I'd like to help keep you anonymous as long as you want to stay anonymous.
I know that speaking your mind in those countries can get you killed, so I don't want to get you in trouble.
But I'll include your letter, you know, because nobody has to know where it came from.
So, you're safe.
But if you want to keep, if you want to have, like, a back-and-forth communication about it, to go to the, you know.
The cool thing about having a back-and-forth over and over again is you can zone in on certain points
And then, and then argue the certain points, the finer details of it, you know.
And maybe you have a certain, maybe we can just change the subject and just focus on one thing
And maybe there's something you want to explain to me that I don't understand, you know.
Or there's something I can further embellish or something like that, so.
So, cool.
But, oh yeah, in the end you said, like, you're a retarded liar and you do retarded videos.
Don't, you shouldn't say people do just stupid videos because you don't want to discourage people from doing YouTube videos
Because the best thing for us is to communicate.
So, we should encourage people to communicate.
That's why I'm, you know, speaking with you and answering all of your points.
If you don't communicate, then you're just going to have, you know, one guy who thinks he knows everything
Telling everybody else what to do, but nobody's talking to him because they can't communicate to him.
So, he doesn't know anything either.
So, it's the blind leading the blind and you're going to, that leads to people making bad decisions for everybody.
So, communication is good and, you know, in that respect, American culture and people doing YouTube videos is a good thing.
And I wish more people in Saudi Arabia would, you know, have the freedom to do YouTube videos
Without fear of getting shot or something like that.
So, all right.
Until next time, peace out.
